coredata.dc:title: Synthesis of biological quantum dots based on single-strand DNA and its application in melamine detection
coredata.prism:publicationName: Spectrochimica Acta Part A: Molecular and Biomolecular Spectroscopy
coredata.dc:creator: Lv, Jinzhi
Liu, Shuying
Miao, Yanming
coredata.dc:description: By taking TC base-rich single-stranded DNA (ssDNA) as the raw material, a fluorescent biological quantum dots (Bio-dots) probe was prepared in one step through hydrothermal method, where its lifetime was greatly extended in comparison with Carbon quantum dots (CQDs), reaching 10.7 ns. The fluorescent detection of melamine in milk samples was realized by using the base pairing principle. Under the optimal conditions, the linear range of Bio-dots probe fluorescence sensor for melamine detection is 5–600 μM, and the detection limit is (3σ) 1.4 μM. Bio-dots can not only emit photoluminescence, but also detect target molecules as a functional recognition group. As the raw material ssDNA was basically non-toxic and there was no toxic substances participated in its synmanuscript process, this Bio-dots probe was a kind of green and environmentally-friendly photoluminescent functional material.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Single-stranded DNA
Biological quantum dots
Melamine

xocs:meta.xocs:srctitle: Spectrochimica Acta Part A: Molecular and Biomolecular Spectroscopy

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Calculation of Bio-dots combined with melamine
Characterization of structural components of Bio-dots
Bonding mechanism of Bio-dots and melamine
Application of Bio-dots to melamine determination

xocs:funding.xocs:funding-agency: National Natural Science Foundation of China

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Schematic diagram of application of DNA-based fluorescent quantum dots to melamine identification in food.

(A) ssDNA (1) (1 μM) and Bio-dots (2) (2.8 mg/L) Absorption spectrum and emission spectra of Bio-dots under different excitation wavelengths; (B) TEM graph of Bio-dots; (C) Fluorescence lifetime of Bio-dots; (D) Effect of pH on Bio-dots (2.8 mg/L); (E) Schematic diagram of Bio-dots luminescence; (E) Representative band structure of bio-dots. The energy levels are quantized with optical bandgap as observed from FL and Uv–vis adsorption characterizaton results. A photoinduced electron-hole pair recombining radiatively is described.

(A) XPS full spectrum of Bio-dots; (B) High-resolution XPS graph of C1s; (C) High-resolution XPS spectrum of N1s; (D) High-resolution XPS graph of O1s; (E) High-resolution XPS graph of P2p; (F) Infrared spectrogram of ssDNA and Bio-dots.

(A) The change of Bio-dots UV absorption with the increase of melamine concentration. (B) RLS change of Bio-dots with the increase of melamine concentration; (C) Fluorescence lifetime after bonding of Bio-dots to melamine (50 μM); (D) Bonding principle between Bio-dots and melamine. (E) Calculation results of docking between thymine and melamine; (F) Docking mode diagram of thymine and melamine. Buffer solution: 10 mM PBS (pH 7.4), Bio-dots (2.8 mg/L).

(A) Change of Bio-dots fluorescence intensity with the increase of melamine concentration. (B) Standard curve for melamine detection via Bio-dots. Buffer solution: 10 mM PBS (pH 7.4), Bio-dots (2.8 mg/L).
Performance comparison among melamine sensors based on different nanomaterials.
Effect of co-existing substance (major biomolecules) on the fluorescence intensity of 50 μM melamine.
Use of Bio-QDs into melamine detection in real samples.

head.ce:title: Synthesis of biological quantum dots based on single-strand DNA and its application in melamine detection

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Shanxi Normal University, Linfen 041004, PR China
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Shanxi Normal University, Linfen 041004, PR China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: By taking TC base-rich single-stranded DNA (ssDNA) as the raw material, a fluorescent biological quantum dots (Bio-dots) probe was prepared in one step through hydrothermal method, where its lifetime was greatly extended in comparison with Carbon quantum dots (CQDs), reaching 10.7 ns. The fluorescent detection of melamine in milk samples was realized by using the base pairing principle. Under the optimal conditions, the linear range of Bio-dots probe fluorescence sensor for melamine detection is 5–600 μM, and the detection limit is (3σ) 1.4 μM. Bio-dots can not only emit photoluminescence, but also detect target molecules as a functional recognition group. As the raw material ssDNA was basically non-toxic and there was no toxic substances participated in its synmanuscript process, this Bio-dots probe was a kind of green and environmentally-friendly photoluminescent functional material.

ce:section.ce:para: Quantum dots (QDs) have been extensively applied to the food analytical chemistry by virtue of its various advantages such as large Strokes shift, superior optical properties and easy functional modification of surface [1–5]. However, the commonly used QDs are prepared by using Cd, Pb, Cu and other elements with high environmental toxicity, or they need high temperature and toxic organic solvent with temperature higher than 100 °C, which limits their practical application. Meanwhile, in order to be hydrophilic or biocompatible, QDs usually need to be surface modified through connection, ligand exchange or other ligand coating to achieve the target recognition performance. The process of functionalizing QDs is not only complex and time-consuming, but also unsatisfactory in stability. Therefore, directly using ligand molecules with low toxicity targeting recognition groups as templates to synthesize QDs sensor with high specificity and stability has become the first choice to solve the above problems.

Designing and preparing new-type functional nanomaterials based on biomolecules is a hot research field in recent years [6–14]. Introducing the biological concepts into the synmanuscript of nanomaterials such as QDs may become an important frontier research direction in Materials Science in the 21st century. However, the research in this field is still in its infancy, which needs to be carried out systematically and in depth. In this manuscript, the construction and preliminary application of nanomaterials based on DNA were studied.

Preparing DNA-dots by taking DNA molecules as the raw material will be of great importance for developing low-toxicity and high-efficiency Bio-dots. Firstly, the diameter of DNA molecules is only 2 nm, which provides potential conditions to prepare special nanostructure. Secondly, as the natural dimension of DNA itself matches with the dimensions of ordinary QDs, it can be used to prepare DNA-dots with proper particle size. Thirdly, nucleic acids are biocompatible and can be directly used as biological functional quantum dots for target recognition in biological samples. Fourthly, nucleic acids with different sequences and functions can be synthesized artificially, which can be used to synthesize DNA-dots with various functions [12,15,16]. Fifthly, nucleic acid degradation products have no environmental toxicity. Sixthly, studies have shown that the carbon like SP2 hybrid center formed by the interaction of cytosine xylene with each other emits light as the luminescence center or chromophore during the synmanuscript of Bio-dots using DNA as the raw material. Additionally, due to the well preservation of the heterocyclic structure of DNA and phosphate group on the skeleton during the formation of such Bio-dots, the strong hydrophilic phosphoric acid group wrapped around the sp2 hybrid carbon center makes a good water solubility of the formed Bio-dots. Hence, DNA is a very ideal biomaterial for nanomaterial synmanuscript because of its strong self-assembling ability, thermodynamic stability, mechanical rigidity and unique topological structure, etc.

As a classical and mature detection method of protein contents, kjeldahl determination method is used to calculate the protein content through nitrogen detection, but its maximum defect is the inability to distinguish whether the nitrogen comes from the protein. Therefore, substances with high nitrogen content have become good substitutes of “proteins”. Since the content of melamine nitrogen is as high as 66.7%, it has become the first choice to be added into milk, and in this case, illegal merchants can not only reduce the cost but also easily reach the stipulated standards. Therefore, melamine has been extensively used as “protein powder” within a considerable long term [17–19]. Melamine itself is of low toxicity and most of which will be excreted with urine after being independently ingested by an adult. However, if it is mixed with cyanuric acid when ingested, it will generate melamine cyanurate, which is indissolvable and able to cause severe kidney stone. Studies indicate that half lethal dose for oral administration of melamine by rats is 3,161 mg/kg, and that for subcutaneous injection of melamine by rabbits is larger than 1 g/kg [20]. Therefore, the development of efficient melamine detection methods for food safety evaluation has an important application value. Although the traditional QDs can realize the sensitive detection of melamine after complex modification [21–25], its preparation process is complex and often contains heavy metal elements or toxic organic compounds. Generally, it needs to be modified twice, and the tested food samples are difficult to be reused, so it is not suitable for food detection. Therefore, the development of lowly toxic or non-toxic melamine detection methods still has important meaning.

In this study, a new green and low toxicity fluorescent Bio-dots probe was prepared by hydrothermal method based on TC base-rich ssDNA, and applied to the identification and detection of melamine in milk sample according to the biological principle of base complementation and pairing (Fig. 1 ).

ssDNA sequences were synthesized by Sangon Biotech (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. Melamine was purchased from Beijing J & K Technology Co., Ltd. Sodium dihydrogen phosphate (NaH2PO4·2H2O) and disodium hydrogen phosphate (Na2HPO4·12H2O) were bought from Tianjin Kermel Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd. Ultrapure water was prepared using WaterPro ultrapure water system (Labconco, US).

The used sequence is as follow:
ssDNA:5′-TC TC TC TC TC TC TC TC TC TC TC TC TC TC TC TC TC TC TC TC TC TC TC TC -3′
T represents thymine and C is cytosine.

Morphological structure of Bio-dots was characterized by Field Emission Transmission Electron Microscope (FEI Tecnai G2 F20 S-Twin Energy Dispersive Spectrometer: Oxford Aztec X-Max 80), and XPS analyzed was performed on X-ray Photoelectron Spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific EscaLab 250Xi). Fluorescence and Resonance Light Scattering (RLS) signals were determined via Cary Eclipse fluorescence spectrophotometer (Varian Inc, US). pH value was measured using a pH meter (Shanghai Leici, China). UV/Visible absorption spectrum (UV/Vis) was determined via UV-29100 UV/Vis (Shimadzu, Japan) UV–visible spectrophotometer. and the infrared spectrum was determined by Varian640 Fourier transform infrared spectrometer (Varian, USA).

The existing method [6] was taken for reference and properly modified to prepare Bio-dots. The preparation process was as follows: 5′-(TC) 24 -3′ssDNA solid powders were centrifuged at 12,000 rpm for 1 min, ultrapure water was added, and the final ssDNA concentration was 10 μM, then followed by 12 h refrigeration so that ssDNA was totally dissolved, after that, the above solution was added into a high-pressure reactor containing PTFE inner tube for sealing and reaction under the temperature of 100 °C for 12 h, and finally the required Bio-dots were obtained through cooling (The concentration of Bio-dots was 140 mg/L).

To investigate the effect of melamine on fluorescence intensity of Bio-dots and realize melamine determination, 500 μL of 0.2 M phosphate buffer solution was firstly added, and then 100 μL of Bio-dots solution and melamine solutions with different concentrations was obtained, the constant volume was 5 mL, after shaking up for 30 min, the fluorescence was detected, with an excitation wavelength of 355 nm.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Calculation of Bio-dots combined with melamine
ce:section.ce:para: In this study, the structure of melamine and thymine was drawn by ChemBioDraw Ultra 17.0, converted into three-dimensional structure by ChemBio3D ultra 17.0, and optimized by MMFF94 force field. Melamine and thymine were converted to PDBQT format using AutodockTools 1.5.6. Autodock vina 1.1.2 was used for molecular docking. Two thymines were attached to melamine. In order to increase the calculation accuracy, the parameter exhaustiveness was set to 20. Unless otherwise specified, all other parameters adopt default values. Finally, the highest scoring conformation was selected and analyzed with Free Maestro 11.9.
Fresh milk from Sannong Fresh Milk Distribution Co., Ltd., Linfen, China, is not processed except diluted.

500 μL of 0.2 M phosphate buffer solution (PBS, pH 7.4), 100 μL of Bio-dots solution and 50 μL of fresh milk (melamine of different amounts was manually added) were added in the colorimetric tube in succession, and the final scalar quantities were 10, 50 and 100 μM, respectively. The concentration of Bio-dots after constant volume was 2.8 mg/L. The fresh milk was diluted by 100 times in the experiment but not treated by other means, and the sample detection steps were the same as those specified in 2.4, finally, the experiment was repeated for 3 times.

Fig. 2 A shows the UV–Vis absorption spectrum and fluorescence spectrum (FL) of Bio-dots. It could be known from the figure that the maximum absorption peak of raw material ssDNA was located at 265 nm, which was generated by free π electrons in thymine and cytosine heterocycles in ssDNA [26]. However, Bio-dots had two different absorption peaks, which were located at 265 nm and 300~400 nm, respectively. Similar to luminescence principles of CQDs, the 300~400 nm absorption band was generated by n-π* electron transition of CO bond [6]. As the excitation wavelength gradually increased from 350 to 385 nm, the emission spectral intensity of Bio-dots firstly increased and then declined at 399–441 nm. The fluorescence value of Bio-dots was the maximum under the excitation wavelength of 355 nm, and the emission peak was located at 405 nm. Furthermore, the emission wavelength depended on the excitation wavelength, possibly because the different types of aromatic fluorescent substances were formed in Bio-dots nucleus, and these different luminescent substances had different excitation wavelengths resulted in the dependence of Bio-dots on excitation wavelength [6,27,28]. Fig. 2B indicates that the particle size of Bio-dots is about 12 nm. As shown in Fig. 2C, the fluorescence lifetime of Bio-dots was 10.7 ns, which was several times to dozens of times than the fluorescence lifetime of common carbon dots [29–31], and more conducive to the analysis and detection of biological samples to reduce the interference of background fluorescence. According to Fig. 2D, Bio-dots were kept relatively stable under pH6.5–9.0. As what was investigated in this paper was biological fluid, pH 7.4 was taken as the experimental condition. In this paper, the light-emitting principle of Bio-dots is shown in Fig. 2E. Through UV–vis and FL (Fig. 2A), we can get that Bio-dots has an optical band gap of 3.5 eV, which should be caused by p and p* states of sp2 carbon centers derived from cytosine located in the band gap of other parts of bio dots, such as PO4 1− group on the main stem. The sp2 carbon centers derived from cytosine play a key role in the Bio-dots luminescent center or chromophore.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Characterization of structural components of Bio-dots
ce:section.ce:para: X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) could be used to characterize the existence form of elements in Bio-dots. Fig. 3 A shows XPS full spectrum of Bio-dots. It could be seen that four characteristic peaks—C, N, O and P—contained by Bio-dots were C1s (285 e V), N1s (401 e V), O1s (532 e V) and P2p (133 e V), respectively, which were consistent with element composition of DNA. Fig. 3B shows the peak-differentiating results of high-resolution spectrum of C1s. It could be known that C1s had four subpeaks, 284.6, 285.3, 286.4 and 288.6 eV, which were corresponding to CC bond on base benzene ring and deoxyribose, CN bond on benzene ring, CO bond in five carbon rings of deoxyribose and keto-carbon CO on benzene ring, respectively, indicating that the existence form of element C in Bio-dots was identical with that of element C in raw material ssDNA, and the carbon morphology of bio dots exposed to ssDNA did not change after the synmanuscript of bio dots by ssDNA. Fig. 3C displays the peak-differentiating results of high-resolution spectrum of N1s, corresponding to NH2, Nand NH, respectively, meaning that the existence form of element N in Bio-dots was consistent with that in raw material ssDNA, and the N morphology exposed to the surface group of Bio-dots did not change after the synmanuscript of Bio dots by ssDNA. The peak-differentiating results of high-resolution spectrum of O1s are shown in Fig. 3D. It could be known from the figure that O1s had four subpeaks, 530.8, 531.4, 532.0 and 532.7 eV, which were corresponding to PO4 1−, OC, ON and OC, respectively, but the existence forms of element O in DNA only included PO4 1−, OC and OC without ON, possibly because the distance between ssDNA molecules was shortened due to the high pressure in the synmanuscript process of Bio-dots, and O/N atoms on adjacent groups piled up on each other [32]. Fig. 3E shows the peak-differentiating results of high-resolution spectrum of P2p. It could be seen that P2p had two subpeaks, 133.4 (2p3/2) and 134.2 eV (2p1/2), manifesting that the existence form of element P in Bio-dots was identical with that of P in raw material ssDNA, and the P morphology exposed to the surface group of Bio-dots did not change after the synmanuscript of Bio dots by ssDNA.

Fig. 3F displays the infrared spectrum of ssDNA and Bio-dots, 1640 cm−1(ssDNA)and 1628 cm−1(Bio-dots) is the stretching vibration peak of T base, 1357 cm−1(ssDNA)and 1353 cm−1(Bio-dots)is the stretching vibration peak of C base, 1271 cm−1(ssDNA)and 1268 cm−1(Bio-dots)is the asymmetric stretching vibration peak of PO2, 1059 cm−1(ssDNA)and 1054 cm−1(Bio-dots)is the symmetric stretching vibration peak of PO2. It can be seen from the above results that the surface structure of Bio dots is basically the same as that of ssDNA after the synmanuscript of Bio dots.

The above analysis results show that, the surface structure of Bio-dots is basically the same as that of raw material ssDNA, and the complete ssDNA sequence is retained on its surface after the synmanuscript of Bio-dots, while the formation of ON bond indicates the successful preparation of Bio-dots. The fluorescence of Bio-dots was mainly ascribed to the formation of sp2 hybrid carbon luminescence center under the dual action of stacked basic groups and ON bond, which was mainly formed by the interaction between cytosine base pairs [6].

ce:section.ce:section-title: Bonding mechanism of Bio-dots and melamine
ce:section.ce:para: Fig. 4 A shows the effect of melamine on UV absorption spectrum of Bio-dots. As the melamine concentration increased, the UV absorption intensity of Bio-dots was gradually enhanced, indicating that Bio-dots and melamine formed larger scattering particles and they were bonded. If the two substances could aggregate through interaction and generate scattering particles, remarkable resonance light scattering (RLS) signal would be generated. The RLS change spectrum of interaction between melamine and Bio-dots manifested that melamine and Bio-dots went through notable aggregation with the addition of melamine (Fig. 4B). Within the wavelength range of 200–700 nm, the RLS intensity of the system was gradually increased (maximum value: 395 nm) with the addition of melamine into Bio-dots solution, indicating that Bio-dots and melamine formed larger nanocomposite particles. Fig. 4C displays the fluorescence lifetime after Bio-dots binding to melamine (50 μM), and the fluorescence lifetime after bonding was lengthened to some extent (fluorescence lifetime of Bio-dots was 10.7 ns), showing that melamine aggregated with Bio-dots to form larger fluorescent particles. Studies have shown that melamine and thymine can be combined by hydrogen bond [33] to further clarify the interaction mode of melamine and thymine at the molecular level. In this study, two thymine molecules are connected to the melamine molecule, and the theoretical calculation combination mode is shown in Fig. 4D, Fig. 4E and Fig. 4F. Two thymine molecules formed a plane with melamine molecule, and each molecule developed three hydrogen bonds with melamine molecule. These hydrogen bond interactions are the most important force between thymine and melamine molecules, which help thymine molecules form stable complexes with melamine molecules, and help melamine molecules form stable complexes with T-ssDNA-PQDs, thus causing quantum dots to generate more effective excitation and finally leading to evident enhancement of Bio-dots fluorescence intensity [34–36].

ce:section.ce:section-title: Application of Bio-dots to melamine determination
ce:section.ce:para:
As shown in Fig. 5 A, the fluorescence intensity of Bio-dots was gradually enhanced in a regular way with the increase of melamine concentration, indicating that Bio-dots could serve as fluorescence probe for melamine detection. Under the optimal conditions, the relation between ratio of fluorescence intensity F of Bio-dots to initial value F0 and melamine concentration conforms to the following equation (Fig. 5B):
F /
F 0
- 1 = 0.0007
Melamine
+ 0.042
R = 0.996

The linear range of fluorescence sensor of Bio-dots for melamine detection was 5–600 μM and the detection limit was (3σ) 1.4 μM. Although the detection limit of Bio-dots for melamine in this study was relatively higher than that of metal quantum dots detection method and electrochemical analysis method (Table 1 ), and the range of detection was broader. More importantly, Bio-dots does not contain metal ions and they are lowly toxic and environmentally friendly.

Some common metal ions and biomolecules in biological fluid were used to investigate the disturbance caused to Bio-dots/melamine reaction system (Table 2 ). Under melamine concentration of 50 μM, 6000 × Na+, 4000 × K+, 100 × Ca2+, 80 × Mg2+, 300 × glucose, 20 × glutamic acid, 50 × alanine, 40 × lysine, 40 × leucine, 40 × arginine and 40 × methionine almost had no effects on the change of fluorescence intensity of this melamine detection system.

To verify whether Bio-dots could be applied in actual biological samples to detect melamine, the melamine content in fresh milk was actually detected in this study, and the standard addition recovery test was also designed. Standard addition-concentrations were 50 μM, 100 μM and 150 μM, and the standard addition recovery rates of samples ranged from 93.37% to 96.9% (Table 3 ).

Taking TC base-rich ssDNA as the raw material, a new-type fluorescent Bio-dots probe was prepared in one step through the hydrothermal method. This Bio-dots probe had completely reserved the basic base structure of raw material ssDNA, with a strong emission peak at 405 nm under the exciting light of 355 nm, and its fluorescence lifetime was lengthened greatly in comparison with that of CQDs, reaching 10.7 ns. Meanwhile, the structure of this Bio-dots probe can also be used as identifying group to identify and detect the target, so Bio-dots have become difunctional quantum dots that can not only emit fluorescence but also serve as basic groups for functional identification. Furthermore, the raw material ssDNA is basically non-toxic, and there is no toxic substances participated in the synmanuscript process. Therefore, Bio-dots probe prepared in this study is a kind of green and environmentally friendly luminescent functional material. Bio-dots were used to identify and detect melamine in milk samples according to the base pairing principles and the detection limit was 1.4 μM. The study results have provided a significant path for developing difunctional Bio-dots.

Jinzhi Lv: Conceptualization, Data curation, Formal analysis, Funding acquisition, Writing - original draft, Writing - review & editing. Shuying Liu: Investigation, Methodology, Project administration. Yanming Miao: Resources, Software, Supervision, Validation, Visualization.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: The work were completed under the support of the National Natural Science Foundation of China (NSFC) (Grant 31700862 and 32001202).

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Graphene oxide quantum dots assisted construction of fluorescent aptasensor for rapid detection of pseudomonas aeruginosa in food samples

sb:title.sb:maintitle: A new class of fluorescent-dots: long luminescent lifetime bio-dots self-assembled from DNA at low temperatures

sb:title.sb:maintitle: One-pot synthesized aptamer-functionalized CdTe:Zn2+ quantum dots for tumor-targeted fluorescence imaging in vitro and in vivo

sb:title.sb:maintitle: One-step synthesis of silver/dopamine nanoparticles and visual detection of melamine in raw milk
